description: Generic taxonomy for extracting a customer DECISION-CRITERIA ledger and typing OBJECTIONS from a transcript. Detection is by linguistic shape/theme only (cue substrings), never a company, product, or specific transcript. A criterion is created only when a category cue matches; criteria_lead_cues merely raise confidence. Objection responses are GENERIC framings (acknowledge + address with evidence) — never an invented product claim, per the sales-intelligence-integrity rule. Logic lives in src/lib/decision-packet; this file is editable without code changes.

objection_types.label: Not a buying motion / out of scope

objection_responses.trust: Acknowledge the credibility concern directly and plan to demonstrate it with the customer's own evidence and explicit confidence / data-gap reporting in the working session — never counter with unproven claims.
objection_responses.commercial: Treat cost governance as a first-class design input (data tiering, retention, scope) and agree what data belongs where before any commitment.
objection_responses.technical: Scope a bounded, credible test on one representative environment rather than promising uniform coverage; validate feasibility with real telemetry.
objection_responses.scope: Reaffirm the boundaries the customer set (no rip-and-replace; preserve specialist tools and systems of record) and frame the work within those constraints.
objection_responses.political: Respect the operating model the customer described (decentralized ownership, existing authoritative systems) and design for shared visibility without central control.
objection_responses.disqualifier: Respect the stated boundary — this is not a qualified buying motion. Do not pursue a sales next step; resolve the actual (support or informational) request through the correct channel.
objection_responses.general: Acknowledge the concern and commit to addressing it with evidence during the agreed working session; do not overpromise.

limitations: Decision criteria are inferred from transcript language; confirm relative priority/weighting with the customer.
Objection responses are framing guidance only — validate specifics against the customer's environment before committing.
